title: Terminal UI Implementations
content:
This module provides lower-level implementations for terminal user interface elements in the Click library. It is designed to be imported only when specific, less frequently used features like progress bars or pagers are needed, to minimize Click's startup time.

Key components:

*   **`ProgressBar`**: A class for displaying progress bars for long-running operations. It supports customization of characters, templates, ETA display, percentage, item display, and width. It can be used as a context manager or an iterator.
*   **`pager`**: A function that intelligently selects a method to display paginated text output, considering environment variables (`PAGER`, `TERM`) and system capabilities (e.g., `less`, `more`).
*   **`_pipepager`**, **`_tempfilepager`**, **`_nullpager`**: Internal helper functions for the `pager` functionality.
*   **`Editor`**: A class to interact with a text editor for editing text content. It can determine the default editor based on environment variables and system commands.
*   **`open_url`**: A function to open URLs or files in the default application or locate them, handling platform-specific commands (`open` on macOS, `start` on Windows, `xdg-open` on Linux).
*   **`raw_terminal`**: A context manager that puts the terminal into raw mode (on Unix-like systems) to capture single key presses without waiting for Enter.
*   **`getchar`**: A function to read a single character from the terminal, with options for echoing the character back to the screen. It handles both Windows (`msvcrt`) and Unix (`tty`, `termios`) implementations.

Usage Example:

```python
import time
from click.termui import ProgressBar

with ProgressBar(range(100)) as bar:
    for i in bar:
        time.sleep(0.1)
```

This will display a progress bar updating every second as the loop progresses.
